rag_rules: Fix type mismatches between component ports by aligning assumed and actual types in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE annexes are placed in correct AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts to avoid unresolved reference errors.
Repair diagnostics by correcting incompatible classifiers in port declarations and ensuring type consistency.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use valid AADL identifiers and types; avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified names.
Guarantees and assumptions should reference only visible features at their scope level.
When fixing type errors, ensure helper declarations match the types of their expressions.
Do not introduce new architecture elements during repair; only correct existing AGREE syntax and references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and types.
Stateful equations require initialized temporal recurrence like `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
Branching logic in AGREE uses nested `if ... then ... else` expressions.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; move them to implementation scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers or types during repair; only use visible ones from the model.
Do not add new architecture elements when fixing AGREE diagnostics.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
Requirement Analyst Agent's role in compact requirement semantics
Model Fusion Agent's role in owner/scope preservation
AGREE logical operators and temporal recurrence patterns
